title: Joby Aviation Shares Pop Nearly 29% as Electric Air Taxi Maker Closes $250 Million Toyota Investment
text: Joby Aviation announced the successful closing of the first $250 million tranche of a strategic investment from Toyota Motor Corporation. This payment is part of a previously announced deal for Toyota to invest $500 million total to support certification and commercial production of Joby's electric air taxis. The total investment from Toyota Motor Corporation in Joby will reach $894 million once completed. The agreement deepens their manufacturing alliance and brings the Toyota Production System into Joby's facilities. Since 2019, Toyota has been investing time and human resources to share its knowledge of the Toyota Production System, with Toyota engineers working side-by-side with the Joby team in California. Toyota and Joby have a long-term agreement for Toyota to supply key powertrain and actuation components. The agreement would allow Toyota to begin low volume commercial manufacturing after Joby receives FAA production certification. Additionally, Toyota signed an agreement that could allow it to build, maintain, repair, and overhaul Joby's air taxi. Joby plans to expand its U.S. manufacturing capabilities, targeting production of four aircraft per month by 2027, with expansion at its California and Ohio facilities.
